name: invoice
description: The money-owed record. Answers 'how much does this customer owe us, for what, and when's it due?'. FUNDAMENTALLY DISTINCT from transaction/payment modules — invoice is a RECEIVABLE (money OWED), transactions + payments are money MOVEMENTS (records of the wire actually being moved). Ships THREE aggregates: Invoice (root record — one per membership renewal or standalone purchase; carries frozen customer address + billing details + due date + status draft/open/paid/partially_paid/void/uncollectible/pastdue/refunded/disputed), InvoiceLine (line items — one per membership charge / add-on / setup fee / late fee / adjustment; carries description + quantity + unit_price + tax_calculation_id + coupon_redemption_id references), CreditNote (reversal invoices for refunds/adjustments — never mutate paid invoices; issue credit note instead). Standard money flow: Membership.creating → Invoice.create (status=draft) → line accumulation → Invoice.finalize (status=open + freeze totals + generate PDF + send email) → PaymentIntent.confirm on the payment module → Invoice.paid (status=paid) → fires marketing::CaptureMembershipPurchasedMarketingEvent when membership_id is set. Multi-currency support: invoice.currency frozen at issuance; conversion rates snapshot when tax + coupon applied. Sequential invoice_number generation per tenant per year (2026-000001 shape) via Postgres advisory lock — required for accounting compliance (GAAP + IFRS mandate sequential numbering). Every state transition audit-logged (owen-it/laravel-auditing via Auditable trait) with 7-year retention (SOX §404 + national tax law). Credit notes are IMMUTABLE reversal invoices with positive amount_cents that represents the credit issued + reference to original invoice + reason enum (refund/adjustment/write_off/duplicate/customer_dispute_resolved/accounting_correction). Sequential credit_note_number per tenant per year (CN-2026-000001). Never conflate with the payment module: invoices know WHAT is owed; payments know WHEN + HOW the money moved. Never conflate with transactions: transactions are the double-entry ledger; invoices are the customer-facing bill. Never conflate with membership.MembershipRenewal: the renewal is the schedule-driven attempt to charge; the invoice is the resulting document.

packages: barryvdh/laravel-dompdf ^3.0 (HTML → PDF renderer for invoice PDFs — server-side, embeds tenant branding via the branding module)
owen-it/laravel-auditing ^13.x (compliance-grade audit trail — every write to invoices/invoice_lines/credit_notes emits an audit row with 7-year retention)
wraps.rationale: PDFs are rendered server-side because (1) accountants + auditors need byte-identical PDFs across time — client-side rendering drifts by browser version; (2) the render must include the tenant's frozen billing details even after tenant branding changes — HTML+DomPDF templates make that trivial; (3) legal jurisdictions (VAT invoices in the EU) require specific field ordering that a template-driven renderer enforces. laravel-auditing is the codebase-standard audit trait — every finance table uses it (see membership + coupon + tax modules).

tenant.description: Tenant-facing invoicing surface. Customer-facing routes (GET /invoices/mine, GET /invoices/{invoice}/pdf, POST /invoices/{invoice}/mark-viewed) let end-users see their own bills. Admin+finance routes (POST /invoices, POST /invoices/{invoice}/finalize, POST /invoices/{invoice}/void, POST /credit-notes) run the finance workflow. Every route carries the `sanctum` guard + `X-Application-Id` header + `entitlement.enforce:invoice_capture`. Customer view is auto-scoped via invoice.enforce_customer_scope middleware — customers only see invoices where customer_id matches their user.

platform-admin.description: Cross-tenant support tooling. Ops + support review invoice status across tenants for escalation cases + refund investigations + compliance audits. Redacted view — customer PII is hashed unless the caller holds platform.invoice.pii.view.
